---
title: "Shawn Graham"
image: "smg-med-upright.jpeg"
links:
  - label: Carleton U
    url: "https://carleton.ca/history/people/shawn-graham/"
  - label: Twitter
    url: "https://twitter.com/electircarchaeo"
  - label: GitHub
    url: "https://github.com/shawngraham"
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:shawn.graham@carleton.ca"
  - label: Notebook
    url: "https://electricarchaeology.ca"
output:
  postcards::trestles
---
 
## Bio

Shawn Graham is a digital archaeologist in the [Department of History at Carleton University in Ottawa, Canada](https://carleton.ca/history). He is also the Programme Coordinator for the [MA specialization in Digital Humanities](https://carleton.ca/dighum). He edits the open access journal [Epoiesen: A Journal for Creative Engagement in History and Archaeology](https://epoiesen.library.carleton.ca/). His current research, with his collaborator Damien Huffer, uses neural networks and computer vision to explore the [online trade in human remains](https://bonetrade.github.io). He is part of the multi-institution SSHRC Partnership Grant funded project, ‘CRANE: Computational Research in the Ancient Near East’ led by Tim Harrison of the University of Toronto. Graham’s sub-project involves using neural networks to complete archival photographs for photogrammetric reconstructions. He is also part of the SSHRC Insight Development Grant funded project ‘Nanohistory‘, led by Dr. Matt Milner of Memorial University, exploring graph-theoretic representations of historical events; his subproject seeks to reconstruct museum collecting histories using the nanohistory method.
His work has been featured in the [Ottawa Citizen](https://ottawacitizen.com/news/local-news/carleton-prof-harnesses-machine-learning-to-explore-the-bone-trade-netherworld/), [WIRED](https://www.wired.co.uk/article/instagram-skull-trade), and [The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2020/11/24/science/artificial-intelligence-archaeology-cnn.html#click=https://t.co/sxyEN0tJtu).

In 2019, he won the [Archaeological Institute of America's Award for Outstanding Work in Digital Archaeology](https://www.archaeological.org/grant/digital-archaeology-award/) for leading the creation of the 'Open Digital Archaeology Textbook Environment', [O-DATE](https://o-date.github.io).

## Education

**University of Reading** | Reading, UK

Ph.D. in Archaeology | 2002

**University of Reading** | Reading, UK

M.A. City of Rome | 1998

**Wilfrid Laurier University** | Waterloo, Ontario

B.A. Hons Archaeology

## Current Courses

**HIST3814o** | [Crafting Digital History](https://craftingdh.netlify.app)  

**HIST3000a** | [Introduction to Digital Archaeology](https://digiarch.netlify.app)

**HIST4916a** | [DH + Museums](https://dhmuse.netlify.app)

**HIST5706a** | [Digital History for Public Historians](#)
